title: Tier identity — per-tier monster pools and visual themes
problem: All tiers share the same monster pool and floor palette. Tiers feel identical past tier 1. There is no visual theme differentiation (e.g. dungeon → crypt → hell) and no tier-exclusive monsters to signal escalating danger.

lineage.spawnReason: deferred scope: tier theming and monster pool segmentation were explicitly cut from the initial implementation

analysis: Monster pool had no progression-tier gating — every tier saw identical enemy types from floor 1. Adding minTier to MonsterDef lets Crypt and Hell tiers surface exclusive harder enemy archetypes while keeping the Normal-tier pool intact for new players. Without this, escalating difficulty tiers felt cosmetic.
implementation: Added optional minTier?: number to MonsterDef and NamedVariant interfaces in src/data/monsters.ts. Added 6 exclusive monsters: Crypt Fiend/Hollow Knight/Grave Warden (minTier 2) and Hell Knight/Doom Guard/Soul Reaper (minTier 3). Updated getMonstersForFloor and getNamedVariantsForFloor to filter by (m.minTier ?? 1) <= tier. Updated spawnMonster signature to accept diffTier = 1 and pass it to both pool functions. gridStore.ts reads useGameStore.getState().tier and passes it into spawnMonster.

decisions.decision: minTier is optional on MonsterDef, defaults to 1 so all existing monsters are unaffected
decisions.rationale: Backwards-compatible — no existing call sites broke. Existing tests continue passing.

decisions.decision: diffTier is a separate parameter from floor, not conflated with floor depth
decisions.rationale: Prevents double-scaling — floor already scales HP/damage, tier is additive on top via applyTierScaling.

decisions.decision: Named variants also gate on minTier so Charnel Fiend (Crypt) and Infernal Knight (Hell) appear only in their tiers
decisions.rationale: Consistency — if a monster base is tier-gated, its named variant should be too.

successCriteria: Floor 1 in Tier 1 shows only Normal-tier monsters. Crypt Fiend appears in Tier 2 but not Tier 1. Hell Knight appears only in Tier 3+. getMonstersForFloor(1, 1) returns no minTier-2 monsters. All 244 engine tests pass.
knownLimitations: No in-game signal that a new tier unlocks different enemy types — players discover organically
Tier 3 early floors have a small pool (only Hell Knight at floor 1) until more tier-3 monsters are added
userGuide: Each difficulty tier (Normal / Crypt / Hell) introduces exclusive enemy types that first appear on floor 1 of that tier. Tier 2 adds armored undead; Tier 3 adds demonic elite enemies with higher base damage. Enemy variety changes automatically — no player action needed.

publicInterface.name: getMonstersForFloor
publicInterface.description: Returns all monster definitions valid for the given floor depth and progression tier.

publicInterface.name: getNamedVariantsForFloor
publicInterface.description: Returns named elite variants valid for the given floor and progression tier.

publicInterface.name: spawnMonster
publicInterface.type: (rng: Rng, floor: number, tier: EncounterTier, diffTier?: number) => MonsterInstance
publicInterface.description: Creates a live monster instance, gating the pool by both floor and progression tier.

implementationNotes: minTier filtering uses (m.minTier ?? 1) so the field is fully backwards-compatible
spawnMonster diffTier default=1 keeps all call-sites outside gridStore forward-compatible

codeSnippets.label: MonsterDef minTier field
codeSnippets.snippet:
export interface MonsterDef {
  id: string
  name: string
  baseHp: number
  baseDamage: [number, number]
  baseXp: number
  minFloor: number
  minTier?: number   // gate on progression tier (1 = always present)
  speed: number
}

codeSnippets.label: getMonstersForFloor with tier gating
spawnMonster diffTier param (gridStore caller)
codeSnippets.snippet:
const diffTier = useGameStore.getState().tier
monster = spawnMonster(rng, floor, tierForEncounter(enc), diffTier)
